steps.title: Identify each text's claim
steps.content:
Text 1: Madhusudhan et al. identified K2-18 b as a hycean candidate, on the inner edge of the HZ (~1 AU).

Text 2: Tsai et al. argue hydrogen-rich atmospheres cause higher temps and water evaporation, pushing the inner HZ edge out to 3.85 AU.

So a planet at ~1 AU is too hot to retain liquid water.

steps.title: Apply Tsai's view to K2-18 b
steps.content:
By Tsai's calculations, K2-18 b sits well inside the new HZ inner edge — its hydrogen-rich atmosphere would drive surface temperatures too high for liquid water.

D captures this exactly: the atmospheric composition implies the surface is unlikely to harbor liquid water. A, B, and C distort or contradict Tsai's argument. D is correct.
